summary: Large item decomp/matching PR touching 30 item-related files. It replaced many item function placeholders with C, expanded item attribute and item-var structs, and updated several prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures. The bot report recorded +8716 matched-code bytes, +48 matched-data bytes, and 51 new matches, with prominent matches in itflipper, ittools, itwhitebea, Game & Watch items, Yoshi egg throw, Hinoarashi, Patapata, and Zgshell.

key_files.role: Expanded character item attributes and item vars, including Climbers Ice, Arwing laser Vec3 fields, Game & Watch parachute attributes, and Hinoarashi structs.
Expanded common item dat attrs/item vars for Flipper, Tincle, Tools, Mewtwo Shadowball, and WhiteBea.
Largest implementation addition; added multiple Flipper functions, collision/physics logic, and two matching handlers guarded by scoped dont_inline plus PAD_STACK.
Added several Tools functions and logic handlers, including spawn/collision/absorbed-related routines.
Added WhiteBea movement/physics routines and used newly expanded WhiteBea attributes.
Added Arwing laser cleanup, animation, effect-spawn, and Vec3 state handling routines.
Implemented it_802C837C item spawn helper and linked spawned item to parent/debug tracking.
Implemented parachute spawn helper and UnkMotion1 animation/removal check.
Added Hinoarashi routines including animation and damage-received logic using new item vars/attributes.
Added item cleanup/destruction routine that clears owner and destroys linked child gobjs with an ItemLink traversal.
Added Mewtwo Shadowball destroy/shield-bounce related logic and angle normalization using M_TAU.
Added Yoshi egg throw spawn/effect-related routines.
Added Patapata animation routine.
Added/changed charge-level outline helper logic using switch-style dispatch.
Added Tincle routine using newly exposed item-var and attribute fields.
Added Zgshell functions including clanked logic and random include.
Added math and itcoll includes needed by Climbers Ice work.
Updated it_802C1854 prototype from unknown to bool(void).
Updated Flipper prototypes for implemented functions.
Updated it_8028EB88 prototype to a typed Item_GObj* signature.
Declared new Game & Watch parachute animation function.
Header updates for Hinoarashi functions.
Added Klap functions with collision/velocity handling.
Prototype cleanup for Klap.
Added db and efsync includes for lipstick spore work.
Prototype cleanup for lipstick spore.
Prototype cleanup for SScope.
Prototype cleanup for Tincle.
Prototype cleanup for Tools.
Small shared item type update.

what_changed: Replaced many item placeholders with C implementations across common and character item files.

Expanded item-specific structs instead of leaving broad padding, including Flipper dat attrs, Tincle item vars/attrs, Mewtwo Shadowball item vars, WhiteBea attrs, Arwing laser Vec3 fields, and Hinoarashi structs.
Updated headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed prototypes where function bodies or call surfaces were added.
Added exact includes needed by recovered code, such as db/db.h, ef/efsync.h, ef/efasync.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, lbvector.h, itcoll.h, and fighter-specific headers.
Bot report recorded 51 new matches and +8716 matched-code bytes, with multiple item functions reaching 100%.
smart_moves: Used typed item-var and dat-attr fields to express offset accesses in headers before or alongside recovered C bodies.
Kept unknown global/function names in address-style form where semantics were not established, while using local role names such as ip, attrs, gobj, parent, pos, and spawn.
Spawn helper implementations consistently filled SpawnItem fields, parent links, facing direction, velocity, and flags before Item_80268B18.
Scoped the Flipper dont_inline workaround with an on/reset pair rather than leaving a broad pragma active.
Arwing laser state used Vec3 assignment for adjacent position fields after header conversion from scalar slots.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When item code repeatedly accesses offsets in xDD4_itemVar or x4_specialAttributes, recovering a narrow item-specific struct can turn raw offset work into readable field access and support matching.
When a placeholder implementation proves a function signature, update the owning header in the same PR rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

Scoped matching tactics such as PAD_STACK and dont_inline appeared only around narrow functions in this slice; intake evidence supports flagging them for verification tracking, not treating them as ordinary style.

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style names such as it_80290C38, it_802C837C, it_802EEFA8, and fn_802C29E8 were retained where semantic names were not proven.
Local variables use common decomp role names: ip for Item*, gobj for Item_GObj/HSD_GObj, attrs for special attributes, parent for parent gobj, pos for Vec3 position, and spawn for SpawnItem.
Unknown recovered struct fields remain offset-based xNN names rather than guessed semantic names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK used in several newly matched functions to control stack shape.
Scoped #pragma dont_inline on/reset used around it_3F14_Logic20_Clanked and it_3F14_Logic20_HitShield.
Explicit SpawnItem field initialization used for item factory helpers before calling Item_80268B18.
Switch statements and exact branch forms appear in recovered state/charge logic, including Arwing laser state cleanup and SScope charge-level handling.
Angle normalization in Mewtwo Shadowball used while loops against 0.0f and M_TAU.

No human review comments or review records were present in the loaded PR slice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed item vars and attributes can unlock broad item matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2317 shows many item matches landing together with expanded item-var/dat-attr structs in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h. Candidate lesson: for item functions, recover narrow xDD4_itemVar and x4_specialAttributes fields before preserving raw padding or offset math.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep speculative item names address-style while using clear local role names
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2317 retained address-style global function names for unknown semantics while using local role names such as ip, attrs, gobj, parent, pos, and spawn.

source_update_candidates.title: Require ledger notes for scoped dont_inline plus PAD_STACK matching
source_update_candidates.text: PR2317 includes a narrow #pragma dont_inline on/reset region around two Flipper handlers and PAD_STACK in multiple newly matched functions. Existing standards already caution that these tactics need evidence; curator may consider adding this PR as an example requiring narrow bot/objdiff evidence and adjacency checks.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete historical example of scoped pragma and PAD_STACK use, but the slice does not include explicit author rationale or objdiff details, so this should be curated as an example only if standards maintainers want one.

source_update_candidates.title: Item implementation/header split in PR2317
source_update_candidates.text: This PR changed item implementations under src/melee/it/items while shared item structs/prototypes were updated in src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h, src/melee/it/itCharItems.h, src/melee/it/items/*.h, and src/melee/it/types.h.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a path-scoping aid for future item decomp, but should be validated against current source ownership before becoming a path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every PAD_STACK or dont_inline use in item code is acceptable; this slice only shows narrow use plus a bot match report.
Do not infer semantic meanings for xNN struct fields from this PR; most fields remain offset-style names.
Do not treat the bot report's unrelated gr/* improvements as intentional PR scope; changed files in this slice are item-related.
